News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Air Strikes Continue Amid Uncertainty Over Iran Peace Plans RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- As the uncertainty surrounding indirect negotiations between Washington and Tehran intensifies, air strikes on Iran, Israel, and surrounding states also continued - "business as usual," as one Israeli military official called it on March 25.
  • Hegseth Relays Death of Iranian Navy Commander, Provides Additional Epic Fury Updates DoD News 26 Mar 2026 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth today relayed the killing of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps navy's top commander by the Israeli military, as part of ongoing Israeli and U.S. kinetic attacks against the Iranian regime during Operation Epic Fury.
  • 'Business As Usual' As Attacks Go On Amid Uncertainty Over Iran Peace Plans RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- As the uncertainty surrounding indirect negotiations between Washington and Tehran intensifies, air strikes on Iran, Israel, and surrounding states also continued -- "business as usual," as one Israeli military official called it on March 25.

  • Statement by PM Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 26 Mar 2026 -- "Last night, we eliminated the Commander of the Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy."
  • Two Senior Leaders of the IRGC Navy Eliminated Israel Defense Forces 26 Mar 2026 -- An IDF precision strike in Bandar Abbas eliminated IRGC Navy commander Alireza Tangsiri and intelligence chief Behnam Rezaei, key figures behind Iran's maritime operations. Their removal deals a significant blow to the IRGC's ability to threaten shipping and destabilize the Strait of Hormuz and the wider region.

  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 26 Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, March 26, 2026, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Army conducted multiple operations as part of Operation True Promise 4, which was launched immediately after the US-Israeli coalition carried out an unprovoked act of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran on February 28.
  • From Tel Aviv to Dimona: Mapping Israeli military and strategic targets of Op. True Promise 4 Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Iranian armed forces and resistance groups across the region continue to carry out retaliatory military operations against the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • Iran's Army drones strike sensitive locations in strategic Haifa port Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army says it has targeted sensitive locations in Israel's strategic Haifa port with drones, almost four weeks after a US-Israeli military coalition began an unprovoked war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran Army says ground incursion would be 'more dangerous and costly' for US Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Iranian Army Ground Force has warned that the forces will "incapacitate" the United States and the Israeli regime if they wage a ground invasion of the country.
  • 'Most extensive': IRGC launches 82nd wave of missile, drone strikes against US-Israeli assets Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced the launch of the 82nd wave of retaliatory operations against the invading US-Israeli coalition, using different types of missiles and drones.

  • Trump Warns Iran To 'Get Serious' But Extends Strait Of Hormuz Deadline RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump postponed threatened attacks on Iranian power plants for a second time, apparently giving Tehran 10 more days to open the Strait of Hormuz to shipping, and said talks on ending the war were going "very well."
  • Shaheen, Schumer, Reed, Warner, Warren Statement on Trump Administration Decision to Ease Iran Oil Sanctions US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 26 Mar 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chuck Schumer (D-NY), Senate Democratic Leader, Jack Reed (D-RI), Ranking Member of the Senate Armed Services Committee, Mark Warner (D-VA), Vice Chairman of the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence, and Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), Ranking Member of the Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee, released the following statement on the Trump Administration's decision to ease Iran oil sanctions:
  • Witkoff Confirms 15-Point Plan Forwarded to Iran Via Pakistan, Claims Positive Reactions Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- The 15-point peace plan widely reported by media is real and has been forwarded to Iran through Pakistan, US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff confirmed on Thursday, further claiming that it has drawn "positive reactions".
  • Trump Says Iran Should Take US Proposal More Seriously Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Thursday that Iran should take the US proposal more seriously, as there would be consequences if the deal was abandoned.
  • Trump Says Iranians Negotiating - But They're Afraid To Admit It RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on March 25 said Tehran was taking part in peace talks, despite official denials, because Iranian negotiators are afraid they will be killed by their "own people."
  • White House threatens to "unleash hell" on Iran People's Daily 26 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump will "unleash hell" if Iran refuses to make a deal over the ongoing U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Wednesday.

  • Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, The Man Washington May Be Talking To In Iran RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- Media reports emerged this week of the Trump administration quietly exploring conservative parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf as a potential negotiating partner - and possibly a future Iranian leader.
  • Qalibaf rejects Trump's ultimatum, says Iran set for 'historic victory' Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has responded firmly to US President Donald Trump's recent threats, asserting that "no one can issue ultimatums to Iran and Iranians."
  • US-Israeli mercenaries captured in four provinces across Iran Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Intelligence Ministry says its forces have identified and arrested 14 mercenaries recruited by the American-Zionist enemy across four provinces who were carrying out activities against national security amid the US-Israeli aggression.
  • Iran prepares 'firm response' to UAE over extensive role in US-Israeli war: Report Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran is planning a decisive response against the United Arab Emirates (UAE) for its instrumental role in both the launch and continuation of the ongoing war against the country, according to a report.
  • Aggressor-linked vessels forfeit right to 'innocent passage' in Strait of Hormuz, Tehran tells IMO Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran's permanent representative to the International Maritime Organization (IMO) Ali Mousavi says vessels not linked to aggressors can enjoy safe passage through the strategic Strait of Hormuz provided that they do not join or support anti-Iran aggression and adhere to regulations set by authorized bodies inside the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran Threatens to Open 'Front' in Bab-el-Mandeb Strait to Counter US - Reports Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran has threatened to open a "front" in the Bab-el-Mandeb Strait to counter the United States, the Iranian news agency Tasnim reported, citing a military source.
  • Iran Allows Passage Through Hormuz for Friendly Countries — Araghchi Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the Strait of Hormuz remains open to friendly states, including Russia, China, Pakistan and Iraq, while closed to its adversaries.
  • Explainer: Why doesn't Iran trust the U.S.? People's Daily 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Tehran Times reported Tuesday that Tehran views U.S. calls for negotiations as a deceptive scheme, stressing that it "does not trust these messages," even as Washington claims progress in talks.

  • Israeli army chief says his forces are on brink of internal collapse amid war against Iran Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Israeli army chief issued an urgent warning to the war cabinet late Tuesday, declaring that the armed forces are on the verge of internal collapse and raising what he described as "ten red flags" over its current condition, according to Hebrew media.

  • Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns UN News 26 Mar 2026 -- The intensifying conflict in the Persian Gulf "has triggered one of the most rapid and severe disruptions to global commodity flows in recent times," the Chief Economist with the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said on Thursday.
  • FAO Chief Economist warns of severe global food security risks from disruption to Strait of Hormuz trade corridor FAO 26 Mar 2026 -- The Chief Economist of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), Máximo Torero, warned that the ongoing disruption to the Strait of Hormuz trade corridor is triggering one of the most severe shocks to global commodity flows in recent years, with significant implications for food security, agricultural production, and global markets.

  • China's Defense Ministry dismisses report of Chinese warning ship near Strait of Hormuz as 'fake news' Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- China's Ministry of National Defense dismissed reports on Thursday claiming a Chinese warning vessel was operating near the Strait of Hormuz, labeling the information "fake news."
  • Chinese FM Cautions Against Strikes on Nuclear Facilities in Middle East Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- Hostilities in the Middle East are intensifying and their consequences are rapidly proliferating, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi warned during a meeting with Rafael Grossi, director general of the International Atomic Energy Agency.
  • Strikes continue and diplomacy persists as Iran war extends; China hopes relevant parties seize opportunity for peace: Chinese FM spokesperson Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- The conflict in the Middle East continues, with US-Israeli strikes on Iran entering its 26th day, while both mixed signs of military attacks and diplomatic mediation emerged.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 26 Mar 2026

  • America's Arab Allies Face Stark Choice In Iran War RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- Caught in the cross fire of the United States and Israel's war with Iran, America's allies in the Persian Gulf face a stark choice: maintain their defensive posture or join the fight against the Islamic republic.
  • For security in a tense global situation: G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting near Paris Germany Federal Foreign Office 26 Mar 2026 -- Wars in Ukraine and the Middle East are dominating the discussions at the G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting near Paris. Read on to find out more.
  • Minister Anand announces additional sanctions against Iran Canada 26 Mar 2026 -- Today, the Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, announced that Canada is imposing additional sanctions against five individuals and four entities under the Special Economic Measures (Iran) Regulations.
  • Backgrounder: Canada imposes additional sanctions against Iran Canada 26 Mar 2026
  • Germany Does Not Want to Be Involved in Middle East Conflict - Defense Minister Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- Germany does not want to be involved in military action in the Middle East, as the US and Israeli conflict with Iran is not its war, German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said on Thursday.
  • Houthi: Yemen ready with all options in solidarity with Iran Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Yemeni leader Abdul Malik al-Houthi has vowed that his country stands ready with all options and will use every means at its disposal to confront the ongoing unprovoked and reckless US-Israeli aggression against Iran.
  • Yemen's Ansarullah ready to enter battlefield in support of Iran: Report Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Yemen's Ansarullah popular resistance movement is reportedly ready to enter the battlefield in support of Iran amid ongoing unprovoked American-Israeli aggression targeting the Islamic Republic.
  • German Defense Chief Calls Iran War 'Catastrophe' To Global Economies RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius on March 26 said a diplomatic solution to the Iran war was needed
  • Korea attends 35-country military talks on Strait of Hormuz KOREA.net 26 Mar 2026 -- High-ranking military officials from 35 countries including Korea on March 26 held a videoconference led by France on restoring navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.
    • "as soon as possible," calling the conflict a "catastrophe."
  • "Operation Desert Shine Successfully Evacuates 204 ROK Nationals" ROK MND 26 Mar 2026 -- According to the Ministries of National Defense and Foreign Affairs, the KC-330 Cygnus aircraft—carrying 211 people, including 204 Koreans, five family members of foreign nationality, and two Japanese nationals—departed Riyadh Airport, Saudi Arabia, on the afternoon of March 14 (local time) and safely landed at Seoul Air Base in Seongnam, Gyeonggi-do, on the afternoon of March 15.
  • "We will never yield to the hate speech, warmongering, and the climate of conflict surrounding us" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 26 Mar 2026 -- Addressing AK Party's Provincial Heads Meeting, President Erdoğan said: "As the state, we will never yield to the hate speech, warmongering, and the climate of conflict surrounding us. Acting with the rightful self-confidence stemming from standing on the right side of history and conscience, we will never lose our calm and common sense. We will never back down from our peaceful foreign policy grounded in peace for all, stability for all, and tranquility for all."

  • Exhausted, Divided, And Waiting: Iranians On A Month Of War RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- Continuing US-Israeli air strikes have left parts of Tehran in rubble and the nerves of many in the capital frayed.
  • Landon Derentz: Markets Expect Quick Off-Ramp From Iran War RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- As the war with Iran continues, access to the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway that sees about one-fifth of the world's gas and oil transit through it, has become an urgent - and uncertain - objective.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Leaders Say Nuclear Forces, Space Domain Paramount to National Security DoD News 26 Mar 2026 -- Protecting America's space systems is essential for national security because so many military capabilities rely on them — including precision navigation, global communications, missile warning and real-time intelligence, said Space Force Gen. Stephen N. Whiting, commander of U.S. Space Command, who testified today in Washington before the Senate Armed Services Committee.
  • Army reaches conditional agreement with private industry for hyperscaled data centers US Army 26 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Army has conditionally selected two companies to enter into exclusive negotiations to build and operate commercial hyperscaled data centers on two Army installations. The initiative demonstrates a model for industry relationships under the Army's Enhanced Use Lease program.
  • U.S. Coast Guard, CNMI partners strengthen maritime security presence in Saipan's waters US Coast Guard 26 Mar 2026 -- Federal and local maritime law enforcement agencies patrolled Saipan's waters and port together in mid-March, boarding six small vessels and launching new coordination channels that expand the CNMI's ability to monitor and respond to threats in its surrounding waters.
  • U.S., allied and partner navies commence Exercise Kakadu 2026 US Navy 26 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Navy forces will join allied and partner nations to participate in Royal Australian Navy-led Exercise Kakadu 2026, a multinational maritime exercise which occurs off Australia's eastern coast, and its International Fleet Review (IFR) starting March 21.
  • Blue Ridge visits Laem Chabang, Thailand US Navy 26 Mar 2026 -- U.S. 7th Fleet flagship USS Blue Ridge (LCC 19) arrived in Laem Chabang, Thailand for a port visit, March 26, 2026. Blue Ridge's last port visit to Laem Chabang was in 2024
  • Pentagon to Skip Global Troop Deployment Review for 1st Time in Decades - Reports Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- The Pentagon plans to withhold its review of US troop deployments abroad for the first time in decades, a document that typically aids Congress and allies in assessing Washington's military priorities and shaping their budgets, media reported, citing US, NATO and European officials.

  • Trump Says Iran Operation Was 'Test' for NATO, Warns US Will Remember Alliance's Actions Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Thursday that the military operation against Iran served as a test for NATO and warned that Washington would remember the alliance's response.
  • Slovenia joins NSPO Ammunition Support Partnership NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) 26 Mar 2026 -- Slovenia has joined the NATO Support and Procurement Organisation (NSPO) Ammunition Support Partnership (ASP), bringing the initiative to 27 participating nations and further strengthening NATO's collective approach to ammunition procurement and defence industrial capacity.
  • NATO Secretary General's Annual Report shows significant increase in defence investment from Europe and Canada NATO 26 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday (26 March 2026), at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte presented his 2025 Annual Report.
  • Press conference by NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte at the launch of the 2025 Annual Report NATO 26 Mar 2026 -- Good afternoon, and thank you for joining us today for the launch of our 2025 Annual Report.

  • Pentagon Mulling Diverting Weapons From Ukraine to Mideast Amid Depleting Stocks - Reports Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- The US Department of War (DoW) is mulling diverting weapons destined for Ukraine to the Middle East as the ongoing conflict with Iran is rapidly depleting critical ammunition reserves, The Washington Post reported on Thursday citing three sources.
  • Two months since the launch of Mission Control within the DELTA ecosystem: the system is now operational across all corps and force groupings Ukraine MOD 26 Mar 2026 -- Personnel across all corps and force groupings are already using Mission Control, the unified digital command-and-control system for drones within the DELTA ecosystem. It is a tool that records all unmanned systems operations and generates structured dashboards for subsequent analysis and decision-making.
  • Latvia and Ukraine sign a letter of intent on support regarding the establishment of a regional cybersecurity center in Ukraine Latvia MOD 26 Mar 2026 -- Strengthening cooperation between Latvia and Ukraine, Minister of Defense Andris Sprūds and the Head of the State Service of Special Communications and Information Protection of Ukraine Oleksandr Potii signed a letter of intent on cooperation between the Ministry of Defense and the State Service of Special Communications and Information Protection of Ukraine in the establishment of a regional cybersecurity center in Ukraine.

  • Regular Press Conference of the Ministry of National Defense on March 26, 2026 PRC MND 26 Mar 2026
  • China unveils urban warfare drill featuring latest generation of robotic wolf units Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- An official media report on Thursday revealed an urban warfare drill featuring the latest generation of robotic wolf units, describing them as having "stronger bodies, smarter brains, and more combat-ready capabilities."
  • America's most pressing task is to earnestly fulfill its special and primary responsibility for nuclear disarmament: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 26 Mar 2026 -- "As a nuclear superpower sitting on the world's largest nuclear arsenal, America's most pressing task is to earnestly fulfill its special and primary responsibility for nuclear disarmament, make substantial and drastic cuts to its nuclear arsenal, and create conditions for other nuclear-weapon states to join the nuclear disarmament process," said a Chinese defense spokesperson on Thursday.
  • Two new types of Chinese anti-drone laser weapons revealed in official media report; expert sees China's laser-based counter-drone technologies at global forefront Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- A report by official Chinese media on Wednesday unveiled two new types of anti-drone laser weapons. A developer said such laser weapon systems, supported by intelligent targeting and multi-sensor integration, demonstrate strong responsiveness and adaptability, while enabling coordinated operations within an integrated combat network.

  • U.S. envoy calls on Taiwan legislators to pass special defense budget CNA 26 Mar 2026 -- The United States' de facto ambassador to Taiwan on Thursday urged lawmakers across party lines to pass a special defense budget to fund planned purchases of U.S. weapon systems for self-defense.
  • Review of Special Defense Act: Procurement Items and Amounts to Be Negotiated ROC MND 26 Mar 2026

  • Australia and Germany to closely cooperate on guided weapons components Australia DOD 27 Mar 2026 -- The Albanese Government has taken a major step toward establishing domestic manufacturing of priority components for anti-ship cruise missiles, delivering another important boost to the nation's guided weapons capability.

  • Canada achieves the 2% of gross domestic product defence spending benchmark Canada 26 Mar 2026 -- The Government of Canada has achieved the North Atlantic Treaty Organization's (NATO) 2% of gross domestic product (GDP) defence spending target in the 2025-26 fiscal year, marking a significant milestone in Canada's approach to national defence and collective security.
  • Prime Minister Carney announces Canada has achieved the NATO 2% defence spending target Prime Minister of Canada 26 Mar 2026 -- "In an increasingly dangerous and divided world, Canada must be prepared - to defend ourselves and our values, to secure our sovereignty, and to stand with our Allies. For too long, Canada did not sufficiently build that strength. By 2014, Canada's defence spending had fallen to just 1% of our GDP, half of our obligations as a NATO member."
  • Prime Minister Carney participates in a virtual meeting of the Joint Expeditionary Force Prime Minister of Canada 26 Mar 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, participated as a special invitee in a virtual meeting of the Joint Expeditionary Force (JEF), hosted by the President of Finland, Alexander Stubb, alongside leaders from Estonia, Iceland, Latvia, Lithuania, the Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, and the United Kingdom, as well as a representative from Denmark.

  • Operation Roaring Lion - Israel MOD Announces 200th Aircraft in Operational Airlift of Military Equipment and Munitions has Landed in Israel Israel MOD 26 Mar 2026 -- As part of the ongoing procurement and shipping effort throughout Operation Roaring Lion, the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) and the IDF's air and sea bridge operations have marked the landing of the 200th cargo aircraft. Since the start of the operation, over 200 aircraft and ships have arrived in Israel carrying approximately 8,000 tons of military equipment, weaponry, and munitions.

  • President Lee hails mass production of homegrown fighter jet KOREA.net 26 Mar 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on March 25 announced a new milestone in the country's self-reliant defense through the start of mass production of the country's first domestically developed fighter jet KF-21.
  • "Intensity of Exercises and Training Determines Combat Power" ROK MND 26 Mar 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back visited a Freedom Shield 26 exercise site and reiterated the importance of a watertight military readiness posture and combined defense posture.
  • "Fully Committed to Building an Advanced, Strong Military Trusted by the People" ROK MND 26 Mar 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back reaffirmed his strong commitment to concentrating capabilities on building an advanced, robust military trusted by the people amid grave security challenges, including those in the Middle East.

  • Joint Statement of Boris Pistorius and Richard Marles Germany - Federal Ministry of Defence 26 Mar 2026 -- In Canberra, Australia, Federal Minister of Defence Boris Pistorius and Australia's Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence Richard Marles issued a joint statement on the international security situation.

  • Joint Statement from the leaders of the Joint Expeditionary Force: 26 March 2026 Gov.UK 26 Mar 2026 -- Leaders of the Joint Expeditionary Force (JEF), met today in Helsinki to reaffirm their commitment to European security and stability, their strengthened support to Ukraine and pressure on Russia, and their continuous effort to respond to regional security challenges together.
  • The President in Helsinki: JEF countries must continue strengthening cooperation with Ukraine while jointly addressing security challenges President of the Republic of Lithuania 26 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, President Gitanas Nausėda participated in the summit of the Joint Expeditionary Force (JEF) countries in Helsinki.

  • Member States expand access to the maritime surveillance network European Defence Agency 26 Mar 2026 -- The European Defence Agency (EDA) and the European External Action Service (EEAS) signed an agreement on 25 March to grant access to the latest version of the maritime surveillance network, known as MARSUR III.

  • OCCAR MUSIS Programme marks First Anniversary of In-Service Support OCCAR 26 Mar 2026 -- The OCCAR MUSIS Programme held the In-Service review meeting at the Embassy of France in Rome to pass the first year of the In-Service Support (ISS) phase of the system.

  • Poland and Switzerland on cooperation in security and civil protection Poland - Ministry of National Defence 26 Mar 2026 -- We have discussed cooperation in the fields of defence, defence industry development and joint training. We have especially focused on advancing cyberdefence capabilities. This is the domain that evolves the most rapidly. Switzerland is a neutral country, but it also has an extremely interesting reservist training and civil defence system - said the Deputy Prime Minister, Władysław Kosiniak-Kamysz after meeting the head of the Swiss Department of Defence.

  • Normal military trade cooperation between the two sides complies with international law, says MND on Serbia purchasing Chinese missiles Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- China and Serbia enjoy friendly relations between their two countries and militaries. Normal military trade cooperation between the two sides complies with international law and common international practice, and is not targeted at any third party, Jiang Bin, spokesperson for Chinese Ministry of National Defense, said on Thursday in response to a question regarding that Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said in a live broadcast that Serbia had recently purchased Chinese CM-400AKG air-to-surface missiles for its air force, and critics claimed the move fuels a regional arms race.

  • Chairman Mast, HFAC, Advances Chip Security Act U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026 -- Today, the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced the Chip Security Act, a measure to prevent the illicit smuggling of advanced AI chips to foreign adversaries as part of a full committee markup.

  • Department of State Launches Pax Silica Fund US Dept. of State 26 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State today announced that it intends, working with Congress, to allocate $250 million in foreign assistance funding for a new Pax Silica Fund initiative to support critical minerals extraction, processing, critical infrastructure, and manufacturing assets that support secure and reliable semiconductor supply chains.

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio Remarks to the Press US Dept. of State 26 Mar 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Bosnia and Herzegovina Chairman of the Presidency Denis Bećirović US Dept. of State 26 Mar 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Jordanian Foreign Minister Safadi US Dept. of State 26 Mar 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Kurdistan Regional Government Prime Minister Barzani US Dept. of State 26 Mar 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Egyptian Foreign Minister Abdelatty US Dept. of State 26 Mar 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 26 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 26 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • 'Merkava massacre': Hezbollah destroys nearly 100 Israeli tanks—$6mn each—in weeks Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- Hezbollah resistance fighters destroyed at least 21 Israeli military Merkava tanks across southern Lebanon and northern occupied Palestine over 24 hours on Wednesday, in what is being described as a new "Merkava massacre."
  • Hezbollah fires record 85 strikes in a day, targets Israeli war ministry with precision missiles Press TV 26 Mar 2026 -- The Lebanese Hezbollah resistance movement has carried out dozens of rocket, missile, and drone strikes against Israeli military installations deep inside the occupied territories within the past 24 hours, setting a new record in the course of retaliatory operations against the Tel Aviv regime.

  • Morocco's Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation, and Moroccan Expatriates, Mr. Nasser Bourita, affirmed on Thursday in Rabat that Morocco considers the West Bank and its stability as prerequisites for the success of any process concerning Gaza. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Iran, Sudan, Cuba UNOCHA 26 Mar 2026

  • Iran war could jumpstart cooperation between Beijing and Manila in South China Sea Radio Free Asia 26 Mar 2026 -- Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz has China and the Philippines talking, but any new oil would be a "hedge for the next crisis."
  • 'Right path': China keen to reopen oil exploration talks with PH Philippine News Agency 26 Mar 2026 -- China has conveyed its openness to restart talks on a possible joint oil exploration with the Philippines in the South China Sea, describing it as the "right path" to upholding stability in the contested waterway.
  • China's stance on 2016 arbitral award, prevarication continues Philippine News Agency 26 Mar 2026 -- China continues to ignore a 2016 ruling of the Permanent Court of Arbitration that dismissed China's sweeping claim over the South China Sea, Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. said.
  • Philippine rights-violating and provocative actions will face more resolute countermeasures: NMD spokesperson Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- The so-called South China Sea Arbitral Award is entirely illegal, null and void. China does not accept or recognize it, and will never accept any claim or action based on the award, said Senior Colonel Jiang Bin, a deputy director-general of the Information Office of the Ministry of National Defense and spokesperson for the ministry, on Thursday in response to claims by certain Philippine Coast Guard officials that relevant waters in the South China Sea is part of Philippine territory based on international law and the "2016 South China Sea Arbitral Award", and any claim that the waters are disputed is a challenge to the Philippines' sovereignty.

  • EUROPEAN UNION RELEASES €250 000 IN HUMANITARIAN AID TO HELP AFGHAN DISPLACED POPULATION European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Mar 2026 -- response to the increasing number of displaced people due to escalating conflict, the European Union is providing €250 000 (over 18 million Afghani) in humanitarian funding to assist the most vulnerable, including those already impacted by last year's earthquakes.

  • DR Congo: violence intensifies as UN mission pledges continued support UN News 26 Mar 2026 -- The security and political situation in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) remains "extremely tense", a senior UN official told the Security Council on Thursday.
  • Despite Ceasefire, Situation in Eastern Democratic Republic of Congo Continues to Deteriorate, Deputy Mission Head Tells Security Council, Citing Civilian Har UN Security Council 26 Mar 2026 -- Despite ongoing mediation and a ceasefire agreed by warring factions, security in the Democratic Republic of the Congo's war-torn eastern region continues to deteriorate, the Security Council heard today, as delegates called for efforts to "bridge the gap" between diplomatic gains and the dire reality on the ground.
  • We condemn increasing drone strikes on civilians and humanitarian workers in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 26 Mar 2026 -- Statement by Jennifer MacNaughtan, UK Minister Counsellor, at the Security Council meeting on the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

  • Minister of International Development Aukrust in South Sudan:"A man-made crisis we cannot ignore" Government of Norway 26 Mar 2026 -- For the first time in four years, a Norwegian minister visits South Sudan. Renewed fighting has led to a sharp deterioration in the humanitarian situation. Minister of International Development Åsmund Aukrust is calling on the country's leaders to engage in dialogue.

  • Polls reset law to boost peace, self-governance in BARMM Philippine News Agency 26 Mar 2026 -- A newly enacted law resetting the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliamentary elections to September 2026 is expected to strengthen peace and self-governance in the region, senators said Thursday.

  • UNICEF Chief calls for urgent global support as children in Somalia face worsening malnutrition UNICEF 26 Mar 2026 -- Rising fuel costs and supply disruptions from war in the Middle East could spell catastrophe for Somalia's children, already reeling from drought, conflict and global budget cuts

News Reports

  • DNI Gabbard Announces Largest-Ever Intelligence Community Cybersecurity Investment and Modernization Effort Director of National Intelligence 26 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard shared a look inside the year-one results of the largest-ever Intelligence Community (IC) wide technology and cybersecurity modernization and investment effort, spearheaded by the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) and carried out in support of President Trump's Cyber Strategy for America.

  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview with France Télévisions public television broadcaster, Moscow, March 26, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026
  • Minister Anand announces additional sanctions against Russia Canada 26 Mar 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, on the margins of the G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting, today announced that Canada is imposing additional sanctions under the Special Economic Measures (Russia) Regulations.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senator Tillis Introduce Bipartisan BLOCK PUTIN Act to Hold Hungarian Government Enablers of Russia Accountable US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 26 Mar 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Thom Tillis (R-NC) introduced the bipartisan Barring Leverage and Obstruction that Contributes to Kremlin Profits Undermining Transatlantic Interests and NATO Act or the BLOCK PUTIN Act, which will hold senior Hungarian government officials accountable if they are obstructing European assistance to Ukraine or aiding the continued reliance of Hungary on Russian oil and gas.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on March 26, 2026 PRC MofA 26 Mar 2026
  • HKSAR Security Bureau tells GT that police must obtain court warrants before accessing devices, rejecting foreign media smears Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- In response to foreign media reports claiming that Hong Kong police can now demand phone or computer passwords from people suspected of breaching the National Security Law (NSL) for Hong Kong, portraying the new rules as a "further crackdown on dissents," a spokesperson from the Security Bureau of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) told the Global Times on Thursday that the reports are misleading, noting that law enforcement officers must apply to the court for a warrant and obtain judicial approval before searching electronic devices such as mobile phones.

  • U.S. lawmakers condemn China over Taiwan's WTO snub CNA 26 Mar 2026 -- Two U.S. lawmakers on Wednesday criticized China for allegedly interfering in the upcoming World Trade Organization (WTO) ministerial conference in Cameroon, which they said led to Taiwan's exclusion from the event.
  • Ex-Taipei Mayor Ko Wen-je sentenced to 17 years for bribery, corruption CNA 26 Mar 2026 -- Former Taipei Mayor Ko Wen-je (柯文哲) was sentenced to 17 years in prison Thursday after being convicted of bribery and corruption charges linked to a real estate development project and three other cases.
  • Ko faces 2028 presidential race ban after receiving 17-year sentence CNA 26 Mar 2026 -- Ko Wen-je (柯文哲), founder of the opposition Taiwan People's Party, faces disqualification from the 2028 presidential election after he was sentenced to 17 years in prison for bribery charges on Thursday.
  • Former Taiwan People's Party chairman Ko Wen-je sentenced to 17 years in jail Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- Former Taiwan People's Party (TPP) chairman Ko Wen-je was found guilty by a court in Taipei in a corruption case on Thursday and sentenced to 17 years in prison, the Xinhua News Agency reported on Thursday.
  • Mainland spokesperson slams Lai administration for manipulating judiciary to suppress political dissent over Ko Wen-je's sentence Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- In response to a media inquiry regarding the comment on the former Taiwan People's Party (TPP) chairman Ko Wen-je, who was sentenced in the first instance to 17 years in prison and six years of deprivation of civil rights on Thursday, Zhu Fenglian, spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said on Thursday that the Lai Ching-te administration, in pursuit of political self-interest, has been blatantly manipulating the judiciary to suppress political dissent, creating "green terror" and practicing a "those who follow will prosper, those who oppose will perish" approach.
  • There is no 'Taiwan Shield,' only 'Taiwan Cage' in front of PLA: MND spokesperson Global Times 26 Mar 2026 -- In front of the PLA, there is no "Taiwan Shield," only "Taiwan Cage." The "Taiwan independence" armed forces seeking to resist like a cornered beast are doomed to destruction, Senior Colonel Jiang Bin, deputy director-general of the Information Office of the Ministry of National Defense and the ministry spokesperson, said on Thursday when addressing a question that Taiwan island is considering building a "Taiwan Shield" modeled on Israel's air defense system, how does the PLA assess such an anti-missile system and from a military perspective, does the PLA believe this anti-missile system can succeed. "Taiwan independence" is a dead end, and China's reunification cannot be blocked, Jiang stressed.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 26 Mar 2026

  • Lukashenko Says Belarus-North Korea Relations Entering New Phase Sputnik 26 Mar 2026 -- Relations between Belarus and North Korea are entering a fundamentally new phase, Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said on Thursday.
  • Ceremony for Welcoming Belarusian President Held KCNA 26 Mar 2026 -- A ceremony took place at Kim Il Sung Square on March 25 to welcome Comrade Alexandr Lukashenko, president of the Republic of Belarus, who is paying an official visit to the DPRK at the invitation of Comrade Kim Jong Un , president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea.
  • Belarusian President Visits Kumsusan Palace of Sun KCNA 26 Mar 2026 -- Alexandr Lukashenko, president of the Republic of Belarus, on an official visit to the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, visited the Kumsusan Palace of the Sun on March 25 to pay his high regard to President Kim Il Sung and Chairman Kim Jong Il .
  • Belarusian President Arrives in Pyongyang KCNA 26 Mar 2026 -- To pay an official visit to the DPRK at the invitation of Comrade Kim Jong Un , president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, Comrade Alexandr Lukashenko, president of the Republic of Belarus, arrived here by his private plane on March 25.

  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (March 26, 2026) ROK MFA 26 Mar 2026

  • Transcript of the Press Briefing by the Spokesperson on Thursday 26th March 2026 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026

  • Minister's meeting with his highness Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (March 26, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026

  • Minister's meeting with Mr Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, India's external Affairs minister (March 26, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026
  • Minister's meeting with Ms Yvette Cooper, Foreign Secretary of the United Kingdom (March 26, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026
  • Meeting between M. Jean-Noël Barrot, Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs, and Mr Toshimitsu Motegi, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Japan (26 March 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026
  • Conversation between the Minister and his counterpart Cho Hyun, South Korean Minister of Foreign Affairs (26 March 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026
  • Meeting between the Minister and Mr Mauro Vieira, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Federative Republic of Brazil (26 March 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 26 Mar 2026

  • Minister Anand meets with France's Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs at G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting Canada 26 Mar 2026

  • G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Paris (Overview of the "Session 1: Global Governance Reform") Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Paris (Overview of the"Session 2: Reconstruction") Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • The Japan-UK Defense Ministerial VTC Ministry of Defense of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • Meeting between Senior Deputy Minister Namazu and Under Secretary Hooker Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • The 3rd Japan-EU Foreign Ministerial Strategic Dialogue Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • Japan: High Representative/Vice-President Kallas holds third EU-Japan Strategic Dialogue with Foreign Minister Motegi European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Mar 2026 -- 26.03.2026
  • Japan-France Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • Japan-UK Foreign Ministers' meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • Japan-Germany Foreign Ministers' Informal Talks Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Mar 2026
  • Japan's Exaggeration of So-called External Threats Is Driven by Ulterior Motives: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 26 Mar 2026 -- "Peace-loving people around the world should unite to resolutely curb the resurgence and spread of 'neo-militarism' in Japan, and to firmly uphold regional peace and stability as well as the safety and well-being of the people," said a Chinese defense spokesperson at a regular press conference on Thursday.

  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Jetten of the Netherlands: 26 March 2026 Gov.UK 26 Mar 2026
  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Kristersson of Sweden: 26 March 2026 Gov.UK 26 Mar 2026
  • PM meeting with President Stubb of Finland: 26 March 2026 Gov.UK 26 Mar 2026
  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Orpo of Finland: 26 March 2026 Gov.UK 26 Mar 2026

  • G7 Foreign Affairs Ministers meeting: press remarks High Representative / Vice-President Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Mar 2026

  • Nigeria-EU 8th ministerial dialogue - Joint communiqué EC/CEU 26 Mar 2026

  • China-Kyrgyzstan Trade Gap Widens As Conflicting Data Raises Transparency Concerns RFE/RL 26 Mar 2026 -- Kyrgyz officials often tout booming trade with neighboring China, but Beijing and Bishkek sharply disagree on the scale of that commerce, according to an analysisof official data by RFE/RL's Kyrgyz Service.

  • World News in Brief, Cuba blockade hits healthcare, Haiti's 'vortex of violence', deadly Djibouti shipwreck UN News 26 Mar 2026 -- Hospitals are just the latest vital public services to be hit in Cuba, more than a month since Washington took measures to block oil supplies from entering the Caribbean nation.
